text:
CYTOPATHOLOGY REPORT

DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID:          CP-XXXXXXXX
Patient:              Kowalski, Raymond
Date of Birth:        [on file]
Medical Record #:     [on file]
Ordering Physician:   Gastroenterology / Interventional Endoscopy
Clinical Service:     Gastroenterology
Attending Pathologist: [Cytopathologist on service]
Cytotechnologist:     [on file]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL HISTORY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
66-year-old male with jaundice, weight loss, and new-onset abdominal pain. Cross-sectional imaging (CT abdomen/pelvis with pancreatic protocol) demonstrated a 3.8 cm ill-defined hypovascular mass in the head of the pancreas with upstream pancreatic ductal dilation, common bile duct dilation, abutment of the superior mesenteric artery, and short-segment involvement of the SMV-portal vein confluence. Serum CA 19-9 markedly elevated at 1200 U/mL. CEA mildly elevated at 4.8 ng/mL. Total bilirubin 2.1 mg/dL. Biliary decompression via ERCP with plastic biliary stent placement performed prior to this procedure. Clinical impression: pancreatic head mass, concerning for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROCEDURE
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Type of Procedure:    Endoscopic Ultrasound-Guided Fine Needle Aspiration (EUS-FNA)
Anatomic Site:        Pancreatic head mass
Procedure Performed By: Gastroenterology / Interventional Endoscopy service
Number of Passes:     4 passes performed with a 22-gauge FNA needle (Cook Medical EchoTip)
Rapid On-Site Evaluation (ROSE): Performed; preliminary assessment yielded cellular material with atypical glandular cells, deemed adequate for further evaluation.
Specimen Submitted:   Smears (air-dried and alcohol-fixed), cell block (formalin-fixed), and rinse fluid

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN ADEQUACY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADEQUATE FOR EVALUATION. Cellular material present on all passes. Cell block sections show adequate cellularity for ancillary studies. No significant obscuring blood or artifact.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Received labeled "EUS-FNA pancreatic head mass" are the following:
  (A) Multiple direct smears — 4 air-dried (Diff-Quik stained) and 4 alcohol-fixed (Papanicolaou stained)
  (B) ThinPrep liquid-based preparation from needle rinse
  (C) Cell block: formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded pellet from needle rinse; 1 paraffin block prepared, sectioned at 4 microns and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E); multiple levels obtained for immunohistochemistry
All material is processed and examined.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CYTOLOGIC FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The smears are highly cellular and show cohesive three-dimensional clusters, crowded sheets, and occasional single atypical epithelial cells set against a background of hemorrhage, proteinaceous material, and scattered desmoplastic stromal fragments. The stromal fragments are composed of dense, hypocellular fibrous tissue, a characteristic feature of the reactive desmoplastic stroma associated with pancreatobiliary-type adenocarcinoma.

The neoplastic cells are large and polygonal to columnar, with markedly enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei. Nuclear features are unequivocal for malignancy and include:
  - Marked nuclear enlargement with nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio greater than 0.7
  - Irregular, angulated nuclear contours with membrane irregularities
  - Coarse, clumped, and unevenly distributed chromatin
  - Conspicuous macronucleoli, often eccentrically placed, present in the majority of cells
  - Marked anisonucleosis (4:1 or greater variation in nuclear size within individual clusters)
  - Occasional nuclear molding
  - Rare mitotic figures identified

Cytoplasmic features:
  - Moderate to abundant pale-to-vacuolated cytoplasm; occasional intracytoplasmic mucin vacuoles present
  - Distinct cell borders where cells form acinar or gland-like arrangements in the three-dimensional clusters
  - Some cells with wispy, ill-defined cytoplasmic tails

Architectural features:
  - Disorganized crowded clusters with loss of honeycomb architecture
  - Sheet-like arrangements with nuclear overlapping
  - Occasional rosette-like or acinar structures
  - Three-dimensional "drunken honeycomb" pattern in some clusters
  - "Picket fence" nuclear palisading at the periphery of some fragments

Background:
  - Desmoplastic stromal fragments (fibrous bands, elongated spindle cells) present — a hallmark of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
  - Rare benign ductal epithelial cells for comparison; the malignant cells show clear-cut morphologic distinction from background benign elements
  - No significant necrosis identified
  - No acinar cell or islet cell features

Cell block sections confirm the above findings. H&E-stained sections of the cell block show infiltrating glandular structures composed of the above-described malignant cells embedded within desmoplastic stroma, closely recapitulating a ductal adenocarcinoma morphology. Gland lumina are irregular and lined by atypical cells.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on cell block sections):

  CK7 (cytokeratin 7):          POSITIVE — diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining
  CK20 (cytokeratin 20):        NEGATIVE
  CDX2:                         NEGATIVE — argues against colorectal or intestinal-type primary
  MUC1 (EMA):                   POSITIVE — strong membranous and cytoplasmic staining
  MUC2:                         NEGATIVE
  MUC5AC:                       POSITIVE (focal-moderate) — consistent with pancreatobiliary lineage
  p53:                          ABERRANT STAINING — diffuse strong nuclear overexpression in >80% of tumor cells, consistent with TP53 gain-of-function mutation
  SMAD4 (DPC4):                 LOSS OF NUCLEAR EXPRESSION (negative in tumor cells; positive internal control in background stromal/endothelial cells) — consistent with SMAD4/DPC4 inactivation, characteristic of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):                Approximately 35-40% nuclear positivity (moderate-high proliferative index)
  Synaptophysin:                NEGATIVE — argues against neuroendocrine differentiation
  Chromogranin A:               NEGATIVE
  CD56:                         NEGATIVE
  TTF-1:                        NEGATIVE
  PAX8:                         NEGATIVE

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY INTERPRETATION:
The immunophenotype (CK7+/CK20-/CDX2-/MUC1+/MUC5AC+/Synaptophysin-/Chromogranin-/TTF-1-/PAX8-) is consistent with a pancreatobiliary-type adenocarcinoma. The pattern of aberrant p53 overexpression and complete SMAD4/DPC4 loss strongly supports the diagnosis of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MMR IHC): RESULT PENDING
  MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 immunostains have been ordered and are in progress. Results will be reported as an addendum.

PD-L1 (22C3 pharmDx assay): RESULT PENDING
  PD-L1 22C3 Combined Positive Score (CPS) to be reported in addendum per oncology request.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CYTOPATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
EUS-GUIDED FINE NEEDLE ASPIRATION, PANCREATIC HEAD MASS:

MALIGNANT

ADENOCARCINOMA, consistent with PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA (pancreatobiliary type).

The cytomorphologic features and immunohistochemical profile are diagnostic of ductal adenocarcinoma of the pancreas. The findings of SMAD4/DPC4 loss and aberrant p53 overexpression further support this interpretation.

See ancillary studies note above — MMR IHC and PD-L1 studies are pending and will be reported in an addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings are unequivocal for malignancy and are consistent with a pancreatobiliary primary (pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma). The complete loss of SMAD4/DPC4 expression, identified in approximately 55% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas, is a diagnostically useful marker in this clinical context and has prognostic implications (associated with a higher propensity for systemic dissemination). The aberrant diffuse p53 overexpression is consistent with a TP53 gain-of-function missense mutation, a common alteration in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

Clinical correlation with cross-sectional imaging, serology (CA 19-9), and multidisciplinary team assessment is recommended.

Specimen retained per institutional policy. Material is available for additional molecular studies if clinically indicated.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (PENDING)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MMR IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2) — PENDING. Expected turnaround: 3-5 business days.
PD-L1 22C3 CPS — PENDING. Expected turnaround: 3-5 business days.
Results will be reported as a formal addendum to this report.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Electronic signature: [Attending Cytopathologist — name redacted for de-identification]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
